title: Bridge Essay: Response-Related Signals Increase Confidence But Not Metacognitive Performance.
concept: Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
**Phase context:** Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

**Response-Related Signals Increase Confidence But Not Metacognitive Performance.** — eNeuro (2020). DOI: 10.1523/ENEURO.0326-19.2020. PMID: 32327471. Confidence judgments are a central tool in metacognition research. In a typical task, participants first perform perceptual (first-order) decisions and then rate their confidence in these decisions. The relationship between confidence and first-order accuracy is taken as a measure of metacognitive performance.
